original_description: Tune NER extraction quality on multilingual chat archives (WhatsApp, Discord). Covers stoplist management, threshold tuning, alias normalization, sender/mentioned separation, cross-source dedup, and precision/recall sampling.
best_description: Tune NER extraction quality on multilingual chat archives (WhatsApp, Discord). Covers stoplist management, threshold tuning, alias normalization, sender/mentioned separation, cross-source dedup, and precision/recall sampling.
final_description:
Use when debugging or improving the output of an existing NER pipeline specifically on WhatsApp or Discord exports — not for building or training models. The skill targets platform-specific failure modes: bot names and usernames leaking into person entities, multilingual honorifics extracted as names, sender/mentioned confusion, stoplist expansion for informal text, alias deduplication across chat platforms, and similarity threshold calibration. 

Triggers on: "my NER keeps extracting Discord usernames as people," "WhatsApp export NER is noisy," "stoplist isn't filtering chat noise," "aliases across Discord exports aren't deduplicating."

Does NOT trigger for: training or fine-tuning any NER model (even on chat data); NER on emails, support tickets, documents, or news; entity extraction tasks not involving WhatsApp or Discord archives.

history.description: Tune NER extraction quality on multilingual chat archives (WhatsApp, Discord). Covers stoplist management, threshold tuning, alias normalization, sender/mentioned separation, cross-source dedup, and precision/recall sampling.

train_results.query: my WhatsApp export NER keeps pulling in 'lol' and 'ok' as person entities, how do I add them to a stoplist
ner on multilingual chat — mix of English and Tagalog — stoplist tuned for English is not catching Tagalog filler words that get tagged as ORG entities
chat archive NER keeps extracting phone numbers from WhatsApp system messages like 'added +1-555-...' — platform metadata leaking in
NER pipeline is mixing up who sent a message vs who they mentioned. 'hey @sarah can you call John' — John showing as sender
aliases are a mess across my Discord exports, 'xX_DarkLord_Xx' and 'Dave' are same user, how do I normalize these before entity dedup
the entity extractor is tagging Discord bot names like 'MEE6' and 'Dyno' as real people, need to filter those out
extracting named entities from customer support email threads to build a CRM — need to identify company names and contacts
want to fine-tune a BERT NER model on our internal chat logs so it learns company-specific names
entity resolution across our CRM and Salesforce — same companies have slightly different names in each system
sentiment analysis on Discord messages — want to track mood trends per channel over time
need to extract person names from news articles and Wikipedia pages for a knowledge graph project
building a NER model eval harness — want to run precision/recall benchmarks across 5 different models on a held-out test set

test_results.query: precision on person entities from our Slack export is 40%, recall is fine. want to do a sample-based audit to find the noise patterns
running spacy on chat archives and threshold 0.6 gives too much noise but 0.8 misses half the real names — how do I tune this
have WhatsApp and Discord exports covering the same group chat, entities are deduped weirdly — 'Mike' vs 'michael.k' are same person
building a social graph from 3 years of family WhatsApp groups, entity extraction keeps surfacing 'papa', 'mom', 'sis' as distinct person entities per group when they should merge
deduplicating chat messages in our Discord export — lots of reposts and cross-channel mirrors creating duplicate message rows
NER pipeline on legal contracts is tagging 'Party A' and 'Party B' as person entities incorrectly
cleaning up our WhatsApp export CSV — removing blank rows, fixing encoding issues, normalizing timestamps before analysis
how do I train spacy's NER component on a custom dataset of medical records

history.description:
Use for improving entity extraction quality on WhatsApp or Discord chat archives — when your NER pipeline is already running but producing noisy or incomplete results. Applies when dealing with chat-specific problems: informal text breaking your entity extractor, username/bot names leaking into person entities, multilingual honorifics appearing as names, sender vs. mentioned confusion, stoplist expansion, alias deduplication across chat platforms, or similarity threshold calibration for fuzzy entity matching.

Not for: training or fine-tuning NER models; NER on formal documents (news, emails, academic text); general data cleaning unrelated to entity extraction; entity extraction from non-chat sources.
